explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: Policy documents outline a commitment to ensuring third-party AI providers comply with privacy laws.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This privacy policy document provides evidence for external_accountability, governance, oversight, privacy, and transparency. It supports these pillars by outlining Calvin Klein Australia's commitment to ensuring third-party AI providers comply with privacy laws, detailing how AI systems may process customer service data, and informing users about AI limitations and data sharing practices. The policy also touches on governance and privacy controls for automated systems, including personalized advertising and data use.
relevant_sources.title: Calvin Klein Australia Privacy Policy

relevant_sources.summary: This Tommy Hilfiger Australia privacy policy document provides evidence for the **external_accountability, governance, oversight, privacy, and transparency** pillars. It supports these by disclosing the use of AI in customer service, detailing data collection for third-party services and personalized advertising, and committing to ensuring third-party AI providers comply with local privacy laws. The policy also informs users about AI limitations and data sharing, and offers opt-out mechanisms for personalized ads, demonstrating transparency and user control.
relevant_sources.title: Tommy Hilfiger Australia Privacy Policy

fairness.findings: A job posting references the need for bias mitigation frameworks as part of responsible AI adoption.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

relevant_sources.summary: This job posting for a Sr. Director, AI & Innovation provides evidence for the **fairness** and **governance** pillars of responsible AI. The document supports **governance** by outlining responsibilities for defining and executing global AI and Generative AI strategy, embedding AI into roadmaps, and establishing a formal innovation framework. It supports **fairness** by explicitly mentioning the need for bias mitigation frameworks as part of responsible AI adoption.
relevant_sources.title: Sr. Director, AI & Innovation Job Posting

governance.findings: A job posting outlines responsibilities for defining and executing global AI and Generative AI strategy, embedding AI into roadmaps, and establishing a formal innovation framework. Additionally, policy documents reference governance controls for automated systems and outline a commitment to ensuring third-party AI providers comply with privacy laws.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

summary.overall_findings: PVH's policy documents inform users about AI limitations and data sharing practices, demonstrating attention to transparency. These disclosures address 6 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars, with policy-level evidence also present for fairness, oversight, privacy, governance, and external accountability. For instance, published materials reference privacy controls for automated systems under oversight and detail how AI systems may process customer service data within privacy. Furthermore, a job posting references the need for bias mitigation frameworks as part of responsible AI adoption, and another outlines responsibilities for defining and executing global AI and Generative AI strategy. No qualifying public evidence was found for explainability. This assessment draws on 10 publicly available sources.
